title: Financial Literacy and Education Commission
summary: This Treasury Department page describes the Financial Literacy and Education Commission (FLEC), established under the Fair and Accurate Credit Transactions Act of 2003 to develop financial education resources. The most recent public meeting was held February 6, 2026, featuring a briefing on 'Trump Accounts' implementation. The content is a government agency landing page without substantive market or macroeconomic analysis.
key_points: FLEC is chaired by the Treasury Secretary and vice-chaired by the CFPB Director, comprising 24 federal agencies.
The commission maintains MyMoney.gov as the federal financial literacy website.
A public meeting was held February 6, 2026 covering 'Trump Accounts' implementation and youth financial education.
The commission publishes annual SAFE (Strategy for Assuring Financial Empowerment) reports to Congress.
No specific policy decisions, economic data, or market-relevant information is presented on this page.
why_it_matters: This page provides no actionable information for market participants. It is an institutional overview of a federal consumer education commission with no relevance to market structure or macro catalysts.
